topic.ja: ぐあいが　わるそうな　ひとに　こえを　かける
topic.en: Checking on someone who looks unwell
topic.de: Jemanden fragen, dem es nicht gut geht
topic.fr: S'enquérir de quelqu'un qui ne semble pas bien
topic.es: Preguntar a alguien que parece estar mal

ja.intro: おとこのひとが　きぶんが　わるそうです。おんなのひとは　なんといいますか。
ja.question: おんなのひとは　なんといいますか。

tr.intro: Erkek öğrenci hasta görünüyor. Kız öğrenci ne söyler?
tr.question: Kız öğrenci ne söyler?

en.intro: The male student looks unwell. What does the female student say?
en.question: What does the female student say?

de.intro: Der männliche Student sieht krank aus. Was sagt die Studentin?
de.question: Was sagt die Studentin?

fr.intro: L'étudiant a l'air malade. Que dit l'étudiante?
fr.question: Que dit l'étudiante?

es.intro: El estudiante parece estar mal. ¿Qué dice la estudiante?
es.question: ¿Qué dice la estudiante?

ko.intro: 남학생이 몸이 안 좋아 보입니다. 여학생은 뭐라고 말합니까?
ko.question: 여학생은 뭐라고 말합니까?

meanings.en: get well soon / take care (said when parting)

meanings.tr: Soru eki — だいじょうぶです (İyiyim) → だいじょうぶですか (İyi misin?)
meanings.en: Question marker — だいじょうぶです (I'm fine) → だいじょうぶですか (Are you fine?)
meanings.de: Fragewort — unterscheidet Aussage (です) von Frage (ですか).
meanings.fr: Marqueur interrogatif — distingue affirmation (です) de question (ですか).
meanings.es: Marcador de pregunta — distingue afirmación (です) de pregunta (ですか).
meanings.ko: 의문문 어미 — です(괜찮아요)와 ですか(괜찮아요?)의 차이.

logic.tr: Ok karakteri endişeli kız öğrencidir ve hasta görünen arkadaşına sormalıdır, bu yüzden '3. だいじょうぶですか' (İyi misin?) doğrudur. Seçenek 1 'だいじょうぶです' soruya verilen cevaptır — hasta olan erkek öğrencinin söyleyeceği sözdür, soran kişinin değil (Rol Karışıklığı). Seçenek 2 'おだいじに' hasta olduğunu zaten bildiğiniz birinden ayrılırken söylenir, henüz fark ettiğiniz anda değil (Bağlam Karışıklığı).
logic.en: The arrow character is the concerned female student who needs to ask about her friend, so '3. だいじょうぶですか' (Are you okay?) is correct. Option 1 'だいじょうぶです' is the reply to that question — it's what the unwell male student would say if asked, not what the concerned person says (Role Confusion). Option 2 'おだいじに' (Get well soon) is said when parting from someone already known to be sick, not when first noticing they look unwell (Context Confusion).
logic.de: Der Pfeil-Charakter ist die besorgte Studentin, daher ist '3. だいじょうぶですか' (Geht es dir gut?) korrekt. Option 1 'だいじょうぶです' ist die Antwort — das würde der kranke Student sagen (Rollenverwechslung). Option 2 'おだいじに' sagt man beim Abschied von jemandem, der bereits krank ist (Kontextverwechslung).
logic.fr: Le personnage avec la flèche est l'étudiante inquiète, donc '3. だいじょうぶですか' (Tu vas bien?) est correct. L'option 1 'だいじょうぶです' est la réponse — c'est ce que dirait l'étudiant malade (confusion de rôles). L'option 2 'おだいじに' se dit en quittant quelqu'un déjà malade (confusion de contexte).
logic.es: El personaje con la flecha es la estudiante preocupada, por eso '3. だいじょうぶですか' (¿Estás bien?) es correcto. La opción 1 'だいじょうぶです' es la respuesta — es lo que diría el estudiante enfermo (confusión de roles). La opción 2 'おだいじに' se dice al despedirse de alguien ya enfermo (confusión de contexto).
logic.ko: 화살표 캐릭터는 걱정하는 여학생입니다. 따라서 '3. だいじょうぶですか'(괜찮아요?)가 정답입니다. 선택지 1 'だいじょうぶです'는 질문에 대한 대답으로 아픈 남학생이 할 말입니다(역할 혼동). 선택지 2 'おだいじに'는 이미 아픈 것을 알고 헤어질 때 하는 말입니다(상황 혼동).

image_prompt: Simple monochrome line art scene inside a Japanese school lecture hall with tiered steps. A female student stands on the steps holding a book, leaning slightly toward a male student with a concerned expression and one hand raised (palm out). The male student sits at a desk on a lower step, head resting on one hand, eyes half-closed, looking tired and unwell, with a textbook on the desk. White background, no shading, clean JLPT exam illustration style. Do NOT include any arrows or numbered labels.
